title: coronilla repanda
intro: Coronilla repanda, also known as the sticky-corydalis or stickwort, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Coronilla repanda include its ability to ease bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Coronilla repanda is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture for herbal remedies, and it may also be used in the form of a salve or ointment for topical applications.
